title: Calibrachoa hassleriana
taxonomy: Calibrachoa hassleriana is a species of flowering plant in the family Solanaceae. It is a member of the Calibrachoa genus, which is closely related to Petunia. Calibrachoa hassleriana is a type of annual flowering plant that is native to South America. The plant is known for its vibrant colors and trumpet-shaped flowers. It is often cultivated in gardens and containers for its attractive blooms.
uses: Calibrachoa hassleriana has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in the treatment of fever and respiratory issues. Its flowers and leaves are said to possess anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also used to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Additionally, it is believed to have a calming effect on the nervous system. Further research is needed to confirm the medicinal efficacy of Calibrachoa hassleriana.

side_effects: Calibrachoa hassleriana, commonly known as the Yellow Trumpet Vine, can be toxic to humans and animals if ingested. Ingestion of the plant can cause gastrointestinal symptoms such as vomiting and diarrhea. Skin contact with the plant's sap may cause minor irritation, but it is generally not serious. If ingested or inhaled in large quantities, it can cause more severe allergic reactions. If you suspect poisoning from Calibrachoa hassleriana, it is essential to seek immediate medical attention.
